Core API

This page covers the abstract interface shared across systems plus the package-wide utility types that are used throughout the guides.

ScQubitsMimic.AbstractQuantumSystem — Type
AbstractQuantumSystem

Top-level abstract type for all quantum systems (qubits, oscillators, circuits). Every concrete quantum system must implement hamiltonian(sys) and hilbertdim(sys).

source
ScQubitsMimic.AbstractQubit — Type
AbstractQubit <: AbstractQuantumSystem

Abstract type for qubit-like systems requiring numerical diagonalization.

source
ScQubitsMimic.AbstractQubit1d — Type
AbstractQubit1d <: AbstractQubit

Abstract type for qubits with a single degree of freedom and a 1D potential (e.g., Transmon, Fluxonium). Supports potential(sys, phi) and wavefunction(sys).

source
ScQubitsMimic.AbstractQubitNd — Type
AbstractQubitNd <: AbstractQubit

Abstract type for qubits with multiple degrees of freedom (e.g., ZeroPi, FluxQubit). Requires multi-dimensional basis handling.

source
ScQubitsMimic.AbstractOscillator — Type
AbstractOscillator <: AbstractQuantumSystem

Abstract type for oscillator-like systems with analytical eigenvalues (e.g., harmonic oscillator, Kerr oscillator).

source
ScQubitsMimic.PhysicalConstants — Module
PhysicalConstants

Collection of physical constants used by ScQubitsMimic.

All values are expressed in SI units unless otherwise noted. The module is used primarily by convert_units and by circuit-energy conversions.

source
ScQubitsMimic.convert_units — Function
convert_units(value, from::Symbol, to::Symbol)

Convert value between energy units. Supported units: :GHz, :MHz, :eV, :meV, :K, :J.

Example

convert_units(5.0, :GHz, :K)   # ≈ 0.24 K
source
ScQubitsMimic.Grid1d — Type
Grid1d(min_val, max_val, npoints)

One-dimensional uniform grid for discretizing phase-space variables. Used by extended-variable operators in the discretized (non-harmonic) basis.

source
ScQubitsMimic.grid_spacing — Function

Return the grid spacing.

source
ScQubitsMimic.grid_points — Function

Return the grid points as a vector.

source
ScQubitsMimic.SpectrumData — Type
SpectrumData

Container for eigenvalues and eigenvectors computed across a parameter sweep.

Fields

  • param_name::Symbol — name of the swept parameter
  • param_vals::Vector{Float64} — parameter values
  • eigenvalues::Matrix{Float64} — eigenvalues[i, j] = j-th eigenvalue at param_vals[i]
  • eigenvectors — optional eigenvectors; nothing if not stored
source
ScQubitsMimic.hamiltonian — Function
hamiltonian(sys::AbstractQuantumSystem)

Return the Hamiltonian as a QuantumToolbox QuantumObject. Must be implemented by each concrete type.

source
ScQubitsMimic.hilbertdim — Function
hilbertdim(sys::AbstractQuantumSystem)

Return the Hilbert space dimension for this system. Must be implemented by each concrete type.

source
ScQubitsMimic.eigenvals — Function
eigenvals(sys::AbstractQuantumSystem; evals_count=6)

Compute the lowest evals_count eigenvalues of the system Hamiltonian.

source
ScQubitsMimic.eigensys — Function
eigensys(sys::AbstractQuantumSystem; evals_count=6)

Compute eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Returns (eigenvalues, eigenvectors).

source
ScQubitsMimic.matrixelement — Function
matrixelement(sys::AbstractQuantumSystem, op, i::Int, j::Int)

Compute the matrix element ⟨ψi|op|ψj⟩ where ψi, ψj are eigenstates. Indices i, j are 1-based.

source
ScQubitsMimic.matrixelement_table — Function
matrixelement_table(sys::AbstractQuantumSystem, op; evals_count=6)

Compute matrix elements ⟨ψi|op|ψj⟩ for all pairs (i, j) up to evals_count. Returns a complex matrix.

source
ScQubitsMimic.set_param! — Function
set_param!(sys::AbstractQuantumSystem, param_name::Symbol, val)

Set a parameter on a quantum system. Default uses setfield!. Override for types where parameters are stored differently (e.g., Circuit).

source
ScQubitsMimic.get_param — Function
get_param(sys::AbstractQuantumSystem, param_name::Symbol)

Get a parameter from a quantum system. Default uses getfield.

source
ScQubitsMimic.get_spectrum_vs_paramvals — Function
get_spectrum_vs_paramvals(sys, param_name, param_vals; evals_count=6, store_eigvecs=false)

Sweep a parameter and compute eigenvalues at each value. Returns SpectrumData. Uses set_param!/get_param dispatch — override these for custom parameter handling.

source
